I really enjoyed

This museum about the gold rush and its impact on Seattle is run by the National Park Service. There is no charge to visit the museum, and the rangers were quite helpful in providing some hints on how best to enjoy our visit. I would recommend watching the 20 minute film in the theater first to get an overview of what is was like during the gold rush days in 1899. The museum has two floors with many signs that can be read along with historical exhibits that really do a good job explaining what life was like for the early miners in Klondike.

We had previously been to Skagway, Alaska - ridden the White Pass and Yukon Railroad up to Carcross, BC. Then been all the way to Dawson City in the Yukon and have seen the gold fields. This was informative to see where the adventure all began in Seattle! Great video - had no idea Nordstrom's began right here during the Gold Rush!
